description: Canonical taxonomy for DevGov AGENTS governance, scope coverage, and queryable instruction indexing.

layers.appliesTo: All agent sessions before file-based instructions are considered.
layers.notes: Recorded as a non-file authority boundary for coverage tests and handoff clarity.

layers.appliesTo: User-level AGENTS instructions outside a specific workspace.
layers.notes: DevGov owns planning, indexing, and validation for this layer while the live Global AGENTS file remains an external runtime input.

layers.appliesTo: Shared workspace overlays above individual repositories.
layers.notes: Used for storage topology, common workspace behavior, and cross-repo conventions.

layers.appliesTo: This DevGov repository and files below its root.
layers.notes: Nearest canonical project policy for DevGov changes.

layers.appliesTo: Future nested instructions for specific folders or generated assets.
layers.notes: Reserved so folder-local policy can be modeled without changing the schema.

layers.appliesTo: The current user request after higher-priority safety and scope rules are satisfied.
layers.notes: Request intent can narrow work but cannot override safety, secrets, or publication boundaries.

itemTypes.label: Scope Layer
itemTypes.description: Defines where an instruction applies in the agent governance stack.
itemTypes.notes: Every durable instruction should be attributable to one layer.

itemTypes.label: Authority Order
itemTypes.description: Explains precedence, override limits, and conflict handling.
itemTypes.governanceUse: Prevents repo-local policy from claiming higher authority than runtime instructions.
itemTypes.notes: Use for priority and conflict-resolution rules.

itemTypes.label: Safety Gate
itemTypes.description: Requires review, backup, read-only handling, or explicit operator intent before risky action.
itemTypes.governanceUse: Controls destructive, irreversible, secret-bearing, or public-exposure workflows.
itemTypes.notes: Safety gates should name the evidence or confirmation expected.

itemTypes.label: Data Contract
itemTypes.description: Defines canonical fields, storage locations, and redaction boundaries.
itemTypes.governanceUse: Keeps registry data stable and reports separated from machine-local evidence.
itemTypes.notes: Use for registry fields, source-of-truth policy, and secret handling.

itemTypes.label: Workflow Control
itemTypes.description: Defines ordered operational steps for scans, applies, repair, cleanup, or publication.
itemTypes.governanceUse: Makes agent execution predictable and review-gated where needed.
itemTypes.notes: Use for process rules that are not registry field contracts.

itemTypes.label: Tool Entry
itemTypes.description: Defines the correct tool or command entry path for a task class.
itemTypes.governanceUse: Prevents accidental use of a broad or unauthenticated tool when a narrower entry exists.
itemTypes.notes: Use for browser, shell, scanner, and dashboard command entry rules.

itemTypes.label: Context Budget
itemTypes.description: Defines prompt, tool, skill, memory, and file-context loading rules that reduce startup or per-task context without weakening safety.
itemTypes.governanceUse: Keeps always-loaded rules small and makes escalation to files, tools, connectors, or skill bodies explicit.
itemTypes.notes: Use for lazy-loading, routing, budget levels, and local context-overhead audits.

itemTypes.label: Verification
itemTypes.description: Defines tests, validators, doctors, or generated artifacts required before completion.
itemTypes.governanceUse: Turns policy changes into observable checks.
itemTypes.notes: Use for required commands and generated evidence.

itemTypes.label: Interoperability
itemTypes.description: Defines alignment points with external governance surfaces such as UniText project-map adapters.
itemTypes.governanceUse: Keeps DevGov records parseable by adjacent tooling without coupling canonical state to another repo.
itemTypes.notes: Use for adapter compatibility and handoff records.

itemTypes.label: External Review Input
itemTypes.description: Captures adopted recommendations from independent reviewers or review perspectives.
itemTypes.governanceUse: Preserves review provenance without turning prose notes into registry policy.
itemTypes.notes: Use only for accepted recommendations that changed a durable rule or verification path.

entries.appliesTo: All DevGov agent work.
entries.requirement: Treat platform, system, developer, and runtime instructions as higher authority than file-based AGENTS content.
entries.enforcement: Document the boundary in AGENTS and keep generated indexes from claiming runtime instructions are fully inspectable.
entries.notes: This is a coverage marker for non-file authority.

entries.appliesTo: User-level AGENTS files when loaded by the runtime.
entries.requirement: Classify global-home rules as applicable context before workspace and repo-local rules.
entries.enforcement: Index as a layer and mark unavailable sources as unresolved rather than inventing rules.
entries.notes: Records the global file layer without storing host-specific paths.

entries.appliesTo: Planning, indexing, validation, and reviewed promotion workflows for Global AGENTS governance.
entries.requirement: Let DevGov manage the Global-layer planning surface while keeping live Global AGENTS edits explicit and review-gated.
entries.enforcement: Generate plans and query artifacts first; require explicit operator intent, reviewed diff, and rollback evidence before changing the live Global AGENTS file.
entries.notes: Promotes DevGov from project-local AGENTS taxonomy owner to home-level Global governance planner.

entries.appliesTo: Canonical records that describe Global AGENTS governance.
entries.requirement: Store stable taxonomy, readiness checks, and evidence anchors without copying host-specific Global AGENTS paths or full runtime instruction contents.
entries.enforcement: Keep raw global-home inspection evidence in reports and promote only reviewed stable records into registry data.
entries.notes: Separates Global-layer management responsibility from ownership of live runtime files.

entries.appliesTo: Workspace-level overlays for shared storage and cross-repo conventions.
entries.requirement: Resolve workspace instructions after global-home and before repo-local rules.
entries.enforcement: Keep workspace-derived facts outside DevGov canonical registry unless explicitly promoted as stable policy.
entries.notes: Supports cross-repo governance without copying local-only evidence.

entries.appliesTo: This repository and its canonical DevGov assets.
entries.requirement: Apply DevGov repo-local instructions to registry, templates, scripts, tests, docs, and reports generated here.
entries.enforcement: Validate repository changes with test and registry checks before reporting completion.
entries.notes: Primary durable policy layer for this project.

entries.appliesTo: Future nested AGENTS files or folder-specific governance documents.
entries.requirement: Let subtree instructions narrow behavior for their folder only and keep parent safety and data contracts active.
entries.enforcement: Model subtree records as narrower layers when they are added.
entries.notes: Reserved layer for future folder-local policy.

entries.appliesTo: The current operator request.
entries.requirement: Use direct requests to select work and narrow scope, not to bypass higher-priority safety or publication controls.
entries.enforcement: Ask only when ambiguity materially changes risk, behavior, or output.
entries.notes: Connects user intent to the rest of the policy stack.

entries.appliesTo: Conflicts between DevGov files and higher-priority instructions.
entries.requirement: Prefer safety, correctness, reversibility, user intent, then speed when rules conflict.
entries.enforcement: Do not encode a repo-local rule that instructs agents to override platform, global, or workspace safety boundaries.
entries.notes: Accepted from the scope and safety review perspective.

entries.appliesTo: AGENTS runtime instructions and optional translated references.
entries.requirement: Treat AGENTS.md as the only authoritative agent-runtime source; AGENTS.zh-tw.md, if present, is human-readable reference only.
entries.enforcement: Do not require a Traditional Chinese companion for AGENTS.md and do not promote translated reference text into canonical runtime policy.
entries.notes: Adopts the operator recommendation that AGENTS files are agent-specialized and should not require a translated authoritative copy.

entries.appliesTo: Registry promotion, apply commands, startup writes, public route promotion, and worktree cleanup.
entries.requirement: Keep audit and scan commands read-only unless a reviewed apply path is explicitly requested.
entries.enforcement: Require backups, reports, or explicit operator intent before mutation paths.
entries.notes: Prevents governance tools from becoming silent mutation paths.

entries.appliesTo: All DevGov canonical registries.
entries.requirement: Store stable policy and reviewed records in registry while keeping machine-local evidence in reports.
entries.enforcement: Reject machine-local paths, secret values, full commands, and temporary evidence from canonical registry data.
entries.notes: Extends the existing registry model to agent instruction records.

entries.appliesTo: AGENTS governance changes that affect broad agent behavior.
entries.requirement: Record accepted review recommendations as actionable policy, not long prose notes.
entries.enforcement: Add only durable conclusions to AGENTS or the registry; keep transient review packets in reports when generated.
entries.notes: Keeps review provenance concise and parseable.

entries.appliesTo: Local scans, browser checks, dashboard startup, and AGENTS index generation.
entries.requirement: Use the narrowest audited command or tool entry that matches the task.
entries.enforcement: Prefer existing npm scripts and DevGov scanners before ad hoc inspection paths.
entries.notes: Aligns command entry with audit-first behavior.

entries.appliesTo: Service startup commands that bind development, dashboard, public-route, or local-tool ports.
entries.requirement: Use a governed-port preflight entry before binding reviewed ports.
entries.enforcement: Prefer scripts/require-governed-port.mjs for startup wrappers so project, service, host, port, protocol, and TCP availability are checked before launch.
entries.notes: Turns the port registry from documentation into an executable startup gate.

entries.appliesTo: Existing project configuration scans, workspace port audits, and governed startup preparation.
entries.requirement: Use scan:project, scan:workspace, and port:preflight as the project settings governance helper path before changing target project port behavior.
entries.enforcement: Parse target project config files as text or JSON only, keep evidence under reports, and do not apply target project edits automatically.
entries.notes: Makes project settings governance a reviewed helper surface instead of an ad hoc scanner.

entries.appliesTo: Global AGENTS, workspace overlays, UniText routing adapters, and ordinary task startup.
entries.requirement: Default to no-tool task routing and escalate only when the current request cannot be completed from safe already-loaded context.
entries.enforcement: Classify tasks into L0 through L4 budget levels before loading files, tools, connectors, or full skill bodies.
entries.notes: Keeps the live global-home surface small while preserving safety and authority rules.

entries.appliesTo: Skill manifests, artifact workflows, connector routing, and generated UniText read models.
entries.requirement: Expose compact skill trigger metadata by default and load full skill instructions only after a task matches the trigger.
entries.enforcement: Store stable trigger, instruction reference, and avoid-when cases; do not copy full skill bodies or local paths into canonical registry data.
entries.notes: Turns skill inventories into a routing index instead of a startup prompt dump.

entries.appliesTo: Context budget governance changes and local prompt-overhead reviews.
entries.requirement: Run the context-budget scanner when auditing observable local prompt overhead.
entries.enforcement: Use npm run scan:context-budget and keep generated context-budget reports under reports as estimate evidence.
entries.notes: Separates measurable local files from runtime-owned platform and connector schema costs.

entries.appliesTo: Lag, timeout, slow browser automation, delayed tool responses, and sluggish UI observations during concurrent local development.
entries.requirement: Classify degraded observations as target-unhealthy, environment-contention, or unknown-degraded before remediation.
entries.enforcement: Compare target-local evidence with shared host pressure and existing DevGov status surfaces before blaming the target project process.
entries.notes: Prevents resource contention from being misdiagnosed as target instability.

entries.appliesTo: Shared resource contention checks across multiple concurrently developed projects.
entries.requirement: Use the existing DevGov dashboard, resource-coordination registry, and on-demand scan before adding new monitoring or coordination services.
entries.enforcement: Prefer npm run scan:resource-coordination or the dashboard resource-coordination API for lightweight current evidence; keep generated snapshots in reports.
entries.notes: Keeps the communication platform light and grounded in existing local mechanisms.

entries.appliesTo: Rolling shared-resource coordination guidance into project AGENTS files.
entries.requirement: Use the thin resource-coordination overlay templates and proposal-only AGENTS scanner before editing a target project AGENTS file.
entries.enforcement: Generate proposal reports with npm run scan:agents -- --agents-file <path>; treat reports as evidence and do not bulk-apply or silently edit target projects.
entries.notes: Keeps cross-project rollout reviewable and prevents the scanner from becoming an apply tool.

entries.appliesTo: Generating Codex memory hint proposals for short-term shared-resource awareness.
entries.requirement: Use the proposal-only RCG memory hint template and scanner output before any reviewed Codex memory update.
entries.enforcement: Generate proposal reports with npm run scan:resource-coordination -- --memory-hint-proposal; do not write real Codex memory from DevGov. Hand reviewed memory-update requests to memory-field or the runtime-owned memory update architecture.
entries.notes: Memory hints are soft awareness only and must not be treated as locks, authoritative current state, transaction records, or scheduling gates.

entries.appliesTo: Browser profiles, DevTools sessions, GPU-heavy rendering, model inference, foreground screen control, pointer, keyboard, simulator, or display use.
entries.requirement: Register intended use before taking capacity-limited or exclusive resources that can interfere with other agents or projects.
entries.enforcement: Publish only sanitized, time-bound claims through the resource-coordination surface or report artifacts; never store secrets, screenshots with private data, full command lines, or credential paths in canonical records.
entries.notes: Turns exclusive resource use into a visible coordination signal without granting unsafe access.

entries.appliesTo: Any real Codex memory update derived from an RCG memory hint proposal.
entries.requirement: Keep DevGov proposal-only; require explicit operator intent and exact JSON review before any external memory architecture writes an RCG hint into Codex memory.
entries.enforcement: Use templates/CODEX.memory.rcg-update-gate.md for review and hand the reviewed payload to the memory-field or runtime-owned memory update surface. DevGov scanners, tests, Doctor runs, dashboard refreshes, and vague OK responses must not write real memory.
entries.notes: Keeps DevGov scanners and Doctor proposal-only while the memory-field architecture owns real memory writes.

entries.appliesTo: Resource snapshots, exclusive-resource claims, communication events, and scheduling decisions.
entries.requirement: Treat stale resource status as historical evidence only and refresh before using it for current congestion, ownership, or remediation decisions.
entries.enforcement: Require generated, observed, refreshed, or expiry timestamps for resource coordination state.
entries.notes: Prevents old claims from blocking current work.

entries.appliesTo: Automatic throttling, pausing, restarting, killing, priority changes, or cross-project scheduling.
entries.requirement: Keep resource coordination observe-first until a separate operator-approved scheduling apply path exists.
entries.enforcement: Do not mutate runtime state or allocate resources automatically through the v1 coordination platform.
entries.notes: Keeps the shared platform from becoming an unreviewed scheduler.

entries.appliesTo: Resource coordination policy changes and contention diagnosis work.
entries.requirement: Run the resource-coordination scanner when producing current shared contention evidence.
entries.enforcement: Use npm run scan:resource-coordination and keep generated snapshots under reports with freshness metadata.
entries.notes: Makes contention diagnosis observable without running a resident monitor.

entries.appliesTo: Local antivirus and endpoint-protection blocks during development builds, tests, dev servers, browser automation, generated binaries, and subprocess spawning.
entries.requirement: Use npm run av:triage as the DevGov entry point before considering any antivirus exclusion.
entries.enforcement: Keep the command dry-run, write evidence to reports, and reject broad scopes or high-risk alert text before producing allowlist candidates.
entries.notes: Gives agents and operators one narrow entry for local antivirus triage instead of ad hoc security setting changes.

entries.appliesTo: Codex and agent workflows that need a standard trigger wrapper for suspected antivirus blocks.
entries.requirement: Use npm run codex:av-hook when alert text, failed command output, or supplied paths indicate antivirus or endpoint-protection blocking.
entries.enforcement: The hook may run the triage command and write command logs under reports, but it must not apply exclusions or change security settings.
entries.notes: Provides a Codex-friendly hook without installing global shell, Git, or antivirus integrations.

entries.appliesTo: Any real antivirus exclusion or endpoint protection setting change after triage.
entries.requirement: Do not apply antivirus exclusions through the v1 triage command; applying a real exclusion requires a separate explicit operator request and fresh evidence check.
entries.enforcement: Triage output may show commented preview commands only and must not disable protection, clear quarantine, restore files, add exclusions, change firewall rules, or modify browser or OS security settings.
entries.notes: Keeps antivirus handling audit-first and prevents dry-run tooling from becoming a silent mutation path.

entries.appliesTo: Completed DevGov batches and AGENTS governance edits.
entries.requirement: Run test, registry validation, doctor, and AGENTS index generation when the change touches agent governance.
entries.enforcement: Report any skipped command and residual risk.
entries.notes: Turns layered policy into executable checks.

entries.appliesTo: Coordination with UniText project-map and governance-folder adapters.
entries.requirement: Generate a queryable text and JSON index that UniText-style tooling can ingest without reading local-only evidence.
entries.enforcement: Keep generated AGENTS indexes in reports and keep canonical item types in this registry.
entries.notes: Adopts the interoperability review recommendation without coupling DevGov to UniText runtime state.

entries.appliesTo: This AGENTS revision.
entries.requirement: Make the non-file authority boundary and review gates explicit before adding automation.
entries.enforcement: Keep scope-layer records and safety-gate records separate so tests can verify both.
entries.notes: Reviewer 1 perspective: instruction boundary, reversibility, and testability.

entries.appliesTo: This AGENTS revision.
entries.requirement: Regularize AGENTS item types so project-map-style tools can query requirements by type, scope, and evidence.
entries.enforcement: Maintain this registry and regenerate the text index after AGENTS governance changes.
entries.notes: Reviewer 2 perspective: parseability, stable taxonomy, and UniText coordination.
